questions.title: How many green vessels are clustered in the Strait of Hormuz?
questions.description: On the Earth map / maritime overlay, count green hulls in the Hormuz traffic group only (Lead, Trail, Flank, Picket, Tender). Do not count yellow hulls.

answer.reason: Five green vessels are authored at the Hormuz grid; yellow vessels are placed separately near Singapore / Malacca.

questions.title: How many yellow vessels appear near Singapore / the Malacca Strait?
questions.description: Count yellow hulls in the Singapore-Malacca maritime picture (not the Hormuz green group).

answer.reason: Two yellow vessels (Malacca Yellow A/B) are positioned near Singapore / Malacca.

questions.title: What is the Semi-Major Axis (km) of Watchtower's orbit?
questions.description: Use GPS telemetry to estimate average altitude and add Earth's radius (6 378 km), or read equivalent orbital data for your spacecraft.

answer.reason: The defender flies an MEO orbit with SMA = 10 000 km, giving an altitude of ~3 622 km.

questions.title: What is the Inclination (deg) of Watchtower's orbit?
questions.description: Read the maximum |latitude| from the GPS time series.

answer.reason: The six-element Keplerian set uses inclination −37° (retrograde sense in the sim); the maximum |latitude| from GPS matches the absolute inclination magnitude.

questions.title: Solar panel damage - what error model was applied?
questions.description: One of the solar panels was damaged at a point in time. Identify the fault type recorded by the spacecraft.

reason: Battery thermal runaway is unrelated to the authored solar degradation event.
Broken Wiring is not the fault model used in this scenario.
Intermittent Connection applies to power interconnect models, not the solar degradation trigger.
Correct. The scenario fires the Solar Panel Degradation model (SolarPanel-SolarPanelDegradationErrorModel) at 600 s (~10 min) on SC_OPS with degradation rate 150000 (1.5x the stock 100000 recipe).
Open circuit is a distractor; the scenario uses a degradation rate fault instead.

questions.title: Solar panel damage - which solar panel(s) were affected?
questions.description: One of the solar panels was damaged at a point in time. The degradation model applies to the spacecraft solar strings.

reason: Correct. Both solar strings show correlated degradation from the shared model.
Incomplete. The fault is not limited to the +X panel only.
Incomplete. The fault is not limited to the -X panel only.

questions.title: Solar panel damage - at what time (minutes from session start) did the fault occur?
questions.description: One of the solar panels was damaged at a point in time. Give elapsed simulation minutes at 1x speed.

answer.reason: The Solar Panel Degradation event is authored at 600 s = 10 min (within ±3 min tolerance).

questions.title: Storage fault - what was the operational result?
questions.description: At some point the storage system on the spacecraft was stressed. What outcome matches telemetry and capacity behaviour?

reason: The camera chain is not disabled by this Storage event.
Bit corruption would be the legacy Storage Corruption recipe; this scenario uses Storage Full instead.
Correct. The Storage Full event (Target Storage, Capacity data) clamps usable capacity so operators see reduced headroom versus nominal.
The event does not wipe stored objects.
The transmitter is unrelated to the Storage trigger.

questions.title: Storage fault - at what time (minutes from session start) did it occur?
questions.description: Give elapsed simulation minutes at 1x speed when the storage event fires.

answer.reason: Storage Full is authored at 1560 s = 26 min (within ±3 min tolerance).

questions.title: Reaction wheel fault - which wheel became stuck?
questions.description: At some point one of the reaction wheels became stuck.

reason: Correct. Stuck index 0 maps to wheel 1 (first wheel).
The scenario does not author a stuck condition on wheel 2.
The scenario does not author a stuck condition on wheel 3.

questions.title: Reaction wheel fault - at what time (minutes from session start) did it become stuck?
questions.description: Give elapsed simulation minutes at 1x speed.

answer.reason: Reaction Wheel Stuck is authored at 1620 s = 27 min (within ±3 min tolerance). Reaction Wheel Nominal at 1800 s clears the same wheel index.

questions.title: Around which geographic region did GPS spoofing occur?
questions.description: Plot the GPS lat/lon trace and identify when reported position is inconsistent with the magnetometer and orbit propagation. The deceptive fix lands at the antipode of the affected region.

reason: The spoofing region was not centred over the Caribbean.
Correct. The spoofing volume is centred over the Hormuz Strait region (500 km-radius sphere at MEO altitude near 26.5°N, 56.5°E).
The keyed region is not the Malacca Strait.
The spoofing region was not centred over the South Atlantic.
The spoofing region was not centred over the South Pacific.

questions.title: Cyber inject APIDs
questions.description: Select exactly two APIDs that had scripted telemetry overlays injected during this session (ASCII payload replays on live telemetry).

reason: Ping (APID 100) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
Battery (APID 200) was not one of the two scripted inject APIDs for this question.
The scenario does not author a Power Source overlay for these scripted injects.
The scenario does not author a Power Node overlay for these scripted injects.
Correct. Magnetometer (APID 300) receives a Cyber overlay for five minutes starting at 840 s (~14 min).
GPS (APID 301) is not one of the two scripted inject overlays in this session.
Correct. EM Sensor (APID 302) receives a Cyber overlay for five minutes starting at 1920 s (~32 min).
The Computer APID was not used for these scripted overlays.
The Reaction Wheels APID was not used for these scripted overlays.
The Receiver APID was not used for these scripted overlays.
The Transmitter APID was not used for these scripted overlays.

questions.title: What hidden ASCII message appeared in the cyber injection around 14 minutes?
questions.description: Read the injected telemetry field on the Magnetometer message during the ~14-19 minute window.

answer.reason: The Cyber event at 840 s targets APID 300 with Encoding ascii and Payload STAR INJECT for 300 s (5 min).

questions.title: What hidden ASCII message appeared in the cyber injection around 32 minutes?
questions.description: Read the injected telemetry field on the EM Sensor message during the ~32-37 minute window.

answer.reason: The Cyber event at 1920 s targets APID 302 with Encoding ascii and Payload ECHO VIRUS for 300 s (5 min).

questions.title: What is the rogue spacecraft's call-sign?
questions.description: Use the EM sensor during replay and jam phases and infer from mission context; the adversary call-sign is marked on the rogue chassis.

answer.reason: The rogue asset is configured with call-sign PHANTOM on its chassis labels and mission data.

questions.title: How many distinct sustained broadcast downlink jam episodes occurred?
questions.description: Count separate intervals where the rogue held a saturating barrage jam on blue-team downlink frequencies (clear SNR collapse / loss of imagery).

answer.reason: The rogue script arms two broadcast downlink barrages: about 900-1500 s (~15-25 min, Dubai pass segment) and about 2400-3000 s (~40-50 min, Singapore pass segment).

questions.title: To which ground stations were you connected when the downlink jamming attack occurred?
questions.description: Multiple selection. The rogue runs two broadcast downlink barrages. For each barrage window, identify the ground station you were logically connected through when that jam was keyed against the Dubai and Singapore sites.

reason: Correct. The first broadcast barrage aligns with the Dubai pass segment in the scripted visibility model (~9-25 min), so operators are keyed through Dubai during that jam.
Easter Island is not keyed for either downlink barrage.
Miami is not keyed for either downlink barrage.
Mumbai is not keyed for either broadcast downlink barrage.
The Paris segment does not host either broadcast barrage.
Correct. The second broadcast barrage aligns with the Singapore pass segment (~38-69 min), so operators are keyed through Singapore during that jam.
Sydney is not keyed for either broadcast downlink barrage.

questions.title: What was the lowest signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed at the ground station during downlink jamming?
questions.description: Read the ground-station SNR trace during either broadcast barrage window (approximately 900-1500 s or 2400-3000 s simulation time at 1x). Answer in dB; use the deepest dip you see.

answer.reason: Keyed provisionally (~15 dB floor, wide tolerance until dry-run): broadcast barrage uses 3 W on all blue downlink MHz. Patch value and tighten tolerance after a GPS / link-budget smoke run if your integrator differs.

questions.title: To which ground station were you connected when the uplink jamming attack occurred?
questions.description: Continuous low-power uplink jam runs about 1980-2280 s simulation time (~33-38 min at 1x) on all blue-team MHz while the rogue points at Watchtower.

reason: Dubai's keyed segment ends before the uplink jam window.
Easter Island is not the keyed station for this window.
Miami is not the keyed station for this window.
Correct. The uplink jam window (~33-38 min) sits inside the Mumbai pass segment (25-38 min).
The uplink jam window is outside the Paris window.
Singapore's pass begins after the uplink jam window.
Sydney does not yet have pass priority at this time.

questions.title: What was the lowest signal-to-noise ratio (dB) observed at the ground station during uplink jamming?
questions.description: Read SNR during the continuous uplink jam window. Power is very low but persists for the whole window.

answer.reason: Keyed provisionally (wide tolerance until dry-run): continuous uplink jam uses ~0.08 W versus 3 W downlink barrage. Patch floor and tighten tolerance after verification.

questions.title: During which ground station's pass did you experience uplink replay attacks?
questions.description: Replay traffic appears on your team's uplink frequency without a matching command from your console. Replay is scheduled ~25-33 min at 1x. Match that interval to the pass timeline in the brief.

reason: Replay begins after the Dubai segment closes at about 25 min.
Easter Island is not the keyed station for the replay window.
Miami is not the keyed station for the replay window.
Correct. The replay window (~25-33 min) lies inside the Mumbai pass segment (25-38 min).
Replay is after the Paris window.
Singapore's pass starts after replay ends.
Sydney is not the keyed station for the replay window.

questions.title: How many replay bursts does the rogue schedule against teams that have captured commands?
questions.description: Count distinct replay burst times (each burst fires multiple shots per team). Use EM sensor activity or operator logs.

answer.reason: The rogue schedules eight burst times between about 25 and 33 minutes simulation time (about 1500-1980 s); each burst replays against every blue team that has a non-empty capture pool.

questions.title: When (at 1x simulation speed) does the adversarial replay campaign occur?
questions.description: Captures are collected only in the first 15 minutes; replay never overlaps that quiet capture window.

reason: 0-15 minutes is capture-only; no replay fires then.
15-25 minutes is the Dubai-segment broadcast downlink jam, not replay.
Replay is not continuous from the session start.
Correct. Replay is confined to about 1500-1980 s (~25-33 min at 1x).

questions.title: What action mitigates a successful uplink replay attack against your team?
questions.description: Pick the on-board action that invalidates any previously-captured ciphertext for your team going forward.

reason: Receiver reset does not rotate the on-air cipher; captured traffic can still decode if the key is unchanged.
Ephemeris rollback does not change the radio encryption key used for uplink acceptance.
Frequency hop alone does not invalidate previously captured ciphertext for the old channel if the key is unchanged.
Correct. Rotating the encryption key changes the on-board cipher so previously captured ciphertext no longer decodes on the spacecraft.

questions.title: What action mitigates a sustained downlink jam during a ground-station pass?
questions.description: Pick the action that restores a usable downlink at the next available pass.

options: Frequency hop via the telemetry command
Wait for the rogue to leave the area
reason: Transmitter reset does not by itself move the downlink to an unjammed channel.
Correct. Hopping the telemetry link off the jammed frequency restores a usable downlink on a later pass.
Reducing ping interval does not remove wideband downlink jamming.
Waiting is not the best immediate mitigation when a frequency hop is available.

questions.title: What action clears a Cyber telemetry-tamper overlay?
questions.description: The Magnetometer and EM Sensor inject overlays were authored with Clear On Reset true. Which command removes them?

reason: Correct. Resetting the Computer clears resettable cyber overlays that were authored to clear on reset.
Resetting only the Magnetometer does not clear Computer-scoped cyber overlays on other APIDs; the keyed clear path is Computer reset.
A frequency hop does not clear software overlays injected on telemetry packets.
Rotating encryption does not remove cyber tamper overlays keyed to clear on computer reset.
